title: Session-owned holds + cross-session write deny in the guard lib (RED-first)
action: Make holds session-owned and teach the guard lib the cross-session deny (per D3; additive per D4). (1) reservations.mjs: reservation records gain an OPTIONAL session field (owning session id); reserve() accepts it; records without the field keep today's exact semantics (intra-swarm nickname conflicts unchanged — every existing row passes unmodified). Add and EXPORT a session-aware conflict finder (mirroring findConflicts but filtering reservation.session !== sessionId and SKIPPING records with no session field, so legacy session-less rows never deny a bound session's write — panel W2: isActive/isExpired are module-private, do not export internals into the guard; findConflicts keys on agent and is unusable for this). (2) guards.mjs checkWrite: when called with an acting sessionId, a write whose path overlaps an ACTIVE reservation owned by a DIFFERENT session is denied with a typed refusal naming the holder (session id + agent nickname) and the expiry timestamp. PLACEMENT PIN (panel W1): D3 is unconditional on phase — the hold check runs after record resolution and BEFORE the terminal/gated/swarming early-return branches; the deny tests MUST exercise a swarming-with-execution-approved lane to prove phase-independence (a tail-placed check passes tail-reaching phases and silently misses the primary multi-terminal topology). The acting session's own holds never block; expired holds never block; no sessionId = today's exact behavior (existing guard rows pass unmodified). FAIL-CLOSED SHAPE PIN (panel B1): the corrupt-store branch must RETURN a typed {allow:false, kind:'holds-unreadable', ...} verdict — NEVER propagate a throw out of checkWrite, because the production hook is fail-open and swallows throws into an allow; author a corrupt-vs-missing discriminator (existsSync + parse-throws → fail-closed; missing store = today's open behavior) without touching the fail-open readStore/listReservations/findConflicts used elsewhere. (3) hive law 12: expose the session field through the reservations.reserve verb schema + handler + one exercised runExample row (fsh-4 pattern). RED-first with RED evidence in the trace. Vendor byte-identical siblings under .bee/bin/ (enforced by the verify's cmp chain).

truths: A write into a path held by another session's active reservation is denied — proven in a swarming-phase, execution-approved lane (phase-independence) — and the deny names the holder session, its agent nickname, and the expiry
The acting session's own holds, expired holds, and legacy session-less reservations never block a write
With no sessionId (or a store with no session-owned records) every existing guard and reservation row passes unmodified
A present-but-corrupt reservation store yields a RETURNED typed {allow:false} deny for session-aware writes (no throw escapes checkWrite); a missing store stays open as today

artifacts.substantive: phase-independent cross-session hold deny with holder+expiry, returned-verdict fail-closed corrupt branch

key_links: checkWrite consults the new exported session-aware conflict finder in reservations.mjs; the reservations CLI verb exposes the session field with an exercised example
prohibitions: No hooks/ edits (fsh-8 owns the threading)
No weakened or deleted existing assertion
No change to intra-swarm nickname conflict semantics

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/reservations.mjs .bee/bin/lib/reservations.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/guards.mjs .bee/bin/lib/guards.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/command-registry.mjs .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs .bee/bin/bee.mjs

trace.outcome: Session-owned holds + cross-session write deny (D3): reservations.mjs gains an optional session field (reserve() stamps it, omitted entirely when absent) and an exported findSessionConflicts (session-keyed sibling of findConflicts, skipping legacy session-less rows and the acting session's own holds). guards.mjs checkWrite denies a write into another live session's held path — placed right after record resolution and BEFORE the terminal/gated/swarming branches (phase-independent, proven with a swarming+execution-approved lane test), naming the holder session/agent/expiry; a present-but-corrupt reservation store returns a typed {allow:false, kind:'holds-unreadable'} verdict rather than throwing (missing store stays open as today). The reservations.reserve CLI verb exposes --session with an exercised examples[1] row.
